title: Minimum insertions or deletions to convert string A to B

content.problemStatement:
We are given two strings, str1 and str2. We are allowed the following operations:

1. Delete any number of characters from string str1.
2. Insert any number of characters in string str1.

Return the minimum number of operations required to convert str1 to str2.
constraints: str1 and str2 contain only lowercase English letters
content.outputFormat: Minimum number of operations required to convert str1 to str2

solution.approach: Use dynamic programming to find the longest common subsequence between str1 and str2, then calculate the minimum number of operations required to convert str1 to str2

code.Python3:
class Solution:
    # Function to find minimum insertions/deletions
    def minOperations(self, s1, s2):
        # Get lengths of both strings
        n = len(s1)
        m = len(s2)

        # Create DP table of size (n+1) x (m+1)
        dp = [[0] * (m + 1) for _ in range(n + 1)]

        # Fill the table using bottom-up approach
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            for j in range(1, m + 1):
                # If characters match, extend LCS
                if s1[i - 1] == s2[j - 1]:
                    dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i - 1][j - 1]
                else:
                    dp[i][j] = max(dp[i - 1][j], dp[i][j - 1])

        # Get LCS length
        lcs = dp[n][m]

        # Return total operations = deletions + insertions
        return (n - lcs) + (m - lcs)

# Driver code
sol = Solution()
s1 = "heap"
s2 = "pea"

# Call function and print result
print("Minimum operations:", sol.minOperations(s1, s2))
